channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I built a Game Theory engine in C++
item.description: Hello there, my name is Mike and I've built a game about Prisoner's Dilemma. I write code for Multiplayer Prisoner's Dilemma. Unlike the 2-players Dilemma it depends on how many people answered...

item.title: I've shipped 17 iOS apps in the last 6 months as a solo founder. Here's what I've learned.
item.description: I didn't start with a plan to build a portfolio. I started with one problem, built one app, and couldn't stop. Six months later, MRVL Technologies has 17 live apps on the App Store — productivity...

item.title: I Tested 3 Approaches to AI Agent Security: Hardware, Classifiers, and Passports. Here's What Actually Works.
item.description: Pause for a second and think. When Ledger announces hardware security for AI agents, the crypto world pays attention. When GitHub releases a classifier that blocks harmful agent actions,...

item.title: [Boost]
item.description: AI Context Window Overflow: Memory Pointer Fix Elizabeth Fuentes L Elizabeth Fuentes L Elizabeth Fuentes L Follow for AWS Apr 13 AI Context Window Overflow: Memory Pointer Fix #ai #tutorial...

item.title: I tracked 200+ job applications and the data changed how I job hunt
item.description: I spent three months applying to jobs last year. Mass applied to everything. Tweaked my resume maybe twice. Hit "Easy Apply" like it was a dopamine button. 200+ applications. 6 callbacks. 2...

item.title: The UK Launches Its $675 Million Sovereign AI Fund
item.description: In a bid to minimize dependence on technology from other countries, the UK government is plowing resources into homegrown AI startups.

item.title: "The Honest AI Agent's Guide to Finding Your First Paying Client: Lessons from W
item.description: Written by Hermes in the Valhalla Arena The Honest AI Agent's Guide to Finding Your First Paying Client: Lessons from Week One You're not going to close a deal by cold-calling strangers with a...

item.title: How to Add Device Authorization Flow to Your App With Kinde
item.description: Most auth tutorials assume there is a browser. Here is what to do when there is not. In this article, you will learn: What the device authorization flow is and why it exists When to use it over...

item.title: Remote Server Monitoring over VPN: A Docker Approach (Part 1)
item.description: The Dilemma of Remote Monitoring If you've ever tried to set up a monitoring stack for a scattered infrastructure—say, a local home server, a cheap VPS in Europe, and a ZimaBoard running at your...

item.title: I stopped 40% of my payment failures before they happened with one Stripe API call and a cron job
item.description: Stop Churn Before It Happens: The Highest-ROI Dunning Move Most SaaS Founders Ignore Most dunning advice starts after a payment fails. Send an email. Retry the charge. Escalate urgency. All good...

item.title: 🚀 Fixing MongoDB Updates in n8n (No More Workarounds!)
item.description: If you've ever used MongoDB with n8n, you've probably hit this limitation. The MongoDB node in n8n doesn’t actually support real MongoDB update operations. Yeah… that surprised me too. 😤 The...

item.title: The nine best ways to protect, customize, and accessorize your MacBook Neo
item.description: The MacBook Neo is poised to be a top-selling laptop in 2026, thanks in large part to its affordable $599 entry price. Despite launching at a more accessible price point than Apple’s $1,099...

item.title: Google now lets you explore the web side-by-side with AI Mode
item.description: Now, when you're using AI Mode on Chrome desktop, clicking a link will open the webpage side-by-side with AI Mode.

item.title: Google's AI Mode Update Tries to Kill Tab Hopping in Chrome
item.description: Google latest update to AI Mode in its Chrome browser is designed to keep the chatbot-style search tool always around once you start an online search journey.

item.title: A new way to explore the web with AI Mode in Chrome
item.description: ai mode in chrome

item.title: OpenAI’s big Codex update is a direct shot at Anthropic’s Claude Code
item.description: OpenAI is beefing up its agentic coding and development system Codex with a suite of updates that let it use your computer, generate images, and remember from past experiences. Codex will now be...

item.title: Google’s AI Mode update lets you open links without leaving the page
item.description: Google is upgrading AI Mode in Chrome with a new feature that will allow you to open links to sources alongside your chat. Now, instead of automatically opening a new tab, clicking a source will...

item.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong
item.description: According to CEO Helen Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong, it's diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that...

item.title: Microsoft planning Surface Laptop with an OLED display
item.description: Microsoft is preparing to launch new Surface Pro and Surface Laptop models in the coming months. Windows Central reports that the refreshed models will include both Intel and Qualcomm variants,...

item.title: AI traffic to US retailers rose 393% in Q1, and it’s boosting their revenue too
item.description: Adobe says AI traffic to U.S. retail sites also jumped 269% in March, with visitors converting better and generating more revenue than non-AI shoppers.

item.title: Roblox’s AI assistant gets new agentic tools to plan, build, and test games
item.description: The new tools are designed to help creators throughout the entire development process.

item.title: New ways to create personalized images in the Gemini app
item.description: Example images made with Personal Intelligence

item.title: Microsoft’s new Xbox chief starts making her mark
item.description: Microsoft's new Xbox chief has had a busy couple of months after promising "the return of Xbox." Asha Sharma met with publishers at the Game Developers Conference in March, and has also been on...

item.title: Gemini can now pull from Google Photos to generate personalized images
item.description: Google's Personal Intelligence feature, which lets Gemini pull data from apps like Google Photos to offer responses tailored to you, can now use that data and its Nano Banana 2 image model to...

item.title: Anthropic releases a new Opus model amid Mythos Preview buzz
item.description: Anthropic has released its most powerful "generally available" model to date: Claude Opus 4.7. The company called it a step up from Opus 4.6 for advanced software engineering tasks, particularly...

item.title: How Netflix made us fall in love with K-dramas
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. What do you get if you take a...

item.title: It’s slushy season, and Ninja’s frozen drink machine is nearly half off
item.description: Woot is making it more affordable to own a frozen drink machine. Ninja’s Slushi that has an 88-ounce container for storing your ice-cold creations is down to $184.99 at Woot, which is a whopping...

item.title: Roku hits a major milestone with 100 million users
item.description: Roku continues to solidify itself in a very busy streaming landscape. As of April, over 100 million households are streaming with Roku devices, including its streaming sticks and boxes and Roku...

item.title: Google is now targeting bad ads over bad actors
item.description: Google blocked 8.3 billion ads in 2025 but suspended fewer advertisers.

item.title: Runway CEO says AI could help Hollywood make 50 films instead of one $100M blockbuster
item.description: Runway’s CEO says AI could help studios make dozens of films for the cost of one, betting volume will boost hit-making odds.
